Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Hardware hotplug events on Linux, the gory details 26 upvotes, 0 comments
  2. Optimizing Recommendation Systems with JDK's Vector API 36 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Kristi Noem Out as DHS Secretary 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  4. Pentagon Formally Labels Anthropic Supply-Chain Risk 45 upvotes, 5 comments
  5. Show HN: Jido 2.0, Elixir Agent Framework 170 upvotes, 38 comments
  6. Let's Get Physical 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  7. A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4k Developer Machines 154 upvotes, 38 comments
  8. Wikipedia in read-only mode following mass admin account compromise 615 upvotes, 187 comments
  9. Google Workspace CLI 854 upvotes, 271 comments
  10. Building a new Flash 689 upvotes, 226 comments
  11. Google Safe Browsing missed 84% of confirmed phishing sites 223 upvotes, 67 comments
  12. Poor Man's Polaroid 165 upvotes, 48 comments
  13. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 328 upvotes, 109 comments
  14. Datasets for Reconstructing Visual Perception from Brain Data 33 upvotes, 6 comments
  15. Fast-Servers 79 upvotes, 25 comments
  16. World-first gigabit laser link between aircraft and geostationary satellite 140 upvotes, 53 comments
  17. The Government Uses Targeted Advertising to Track Your Location 156 upvotes, 62 comments
  18. Greg Kroah-Hartman Stretches Support Periods for Key Linux LTS Kernels 37 upvotes, 14 comments
  19. Good software knows when to stop 229 upvotes, 139 comments
  20. US asked Ukraine for help fighting Iranian drones, Zelensky says 83 upvotes, 44 comments
  21. The Man Who Broke into Jail 60 upvotes, 35 comments
  22. Show HN: PageAgent, A GUI agent that lives inside your web app 44 upvotes, 25 comments

  24. Remotely unlocking an encrypted hard disk 4 upvotes, 0 comments
  25. The Brand Age 68 upvotes, 51 comments
  26. AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 202 upvotes, 189 comments
  27. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 339 upvotes, 335 comments
  28. GPT-5.4 209 upvotes, 219 comments
